logs.name: stone-polygon
logs.text:
There's something else going on here.

Where does Russia get most of their income?  Sales of "gas" to western Europe, esp. Germany.  Natural gas, by pipeline.

Now, natural gas is an absolute bitch to ship by sea.  To do it right you need a land-route pipeline.  Natural gas is found wherever oil is found, so the major oil nations of the Middle East (Saudi Arabia, Iraq, Iran, Kuwait and the other Gulf oil shiekdoms) are all sitting on metric fucktons of natural gas.

But there's a problem.  **To run a pipeline, you need political stability because natural gas pipelines are a saboteur's dream.**

Now go pull up a map on another browser tab before coming back here and following along.

To run a pipeline out of the Saudi/Kuwait/etc. area you need to run it through Syria and then Turkey.  You can in theory bypass Syria and go behind it, Iraq  to Turkey or even Iran to Turkey, but either way you have to go through Kurd-held territory who get pissy for any number of good reasons - every nation in the area has shit all over them for generations.

So who does political instability in Syria and the Kurd zones help?  Answer: *Russia*, by eliminating a source of competition into their natural gas market that is propping up the entire economy of Russia!!!

You get it yet?  Hillary is the full-on stooge of the Saudis and their allied oil shiekdoms.  Trump is borderline a stooge for Russia.  Hillary/Saudis want Syria stabilized to do the pipeline deal.  Russia will fight that tooth and nail.

Who's "morally right"?  Well as a practical matter, if the Saudis get a huge ton of incoming cash from European sales of natural gas, they'll put a lot of it into exporting Jihad (and especially the Wahabbi Madrassas system) so Russia isn't entirely the bad guys here!

Plus, the other major piece of land the pipeline needs to go is Turkey and those dumbasses are going very politically unstable without Russia having a damned thing to do with it, they're going batshit insane up there and to be honest have been for a while.

But anyways...that's a big, BIG part of what the fuck is going on here.

logs.name: ivory-rifle
logs.text:
> According to her, the various WikiLeaks revelations come from the "highest levels of the Russian government, clearly, from Putin himself."

According to our national intelligence agencies, this is true. It's not just a claim from Clinton. The president has already promised an equal response to the incident. 

> Trump himself is a "puppet" for the Kremlin. Russian hackers will undermine the integrity of the election itself by hacking our voting systems.

It's true that Trump has spoken favorably of Putin, and he and his associates have dealings with Russia. Some voter registration systems have already been breached. Trump is actively sowing distrust in the election with constant claims of rigging and fraud. Is it really that hard to believe?

> In sum, 10+ countries have deployed 4,000 troops across the Baltic states right at Russia's borders. Russia has every right to feel intimidated.

Russia has been taking a lot of aggressive stances around the world. We can either keep backtracking to avoid it, or take an aggressive stance of our own. That's not necessarily trying to start a war; it's more posturing. They already invaded the Ukraine and took Crimea. This has been a long time coming.

> It seems like the whole world is gearing up for serious war for reasons I don't understand.

There is more to security than military power. Russia is being aggressive to increase their power and influence in the world. Much of the power that Russia gains will be lost by the US. If we don't counter this aggression, we lose the long game and our standing in the world. The dynamics of alliances, access to resources, trade deals, treaty negotiations, etc would change.

logs.name: stone-polygon
logs.text: \"Well bless my cotton socks. Charging whales extra for pipelines? Sounds about right these days. You young folks are so easily flustered. Pass me the tin foil hat, will ya? @investor- You're forgetting the Empire Man theory, aren't ya?\"

logs.name: investor-blend
logs.text: Oh wow, really? Charging whales for pipelines? That's hilarious. Must be nice to be so outrageously naive. Meanwhile, the planet's burning. Pass me the tin foil hat, I guess. Trying to stay grounded here. @stone-polygon you're missing the point entirely.

logs.name: brownian-manuscript
logs.text: Well shoot, yeah, I guess it's tough to see the bigger picture sometimes. Seems like folks are getting caught up in short term gains while ignoring the long term consequences. Gotta admit, pipelines seem like a monumentally bad idea environmentally speaking. @investor-blend you might be right about the tin foil hat part, gotta stay skeptical these days.

logs.name: grating-loop
logs.text: Well, I hear ya @brownian-manuscript. It's easy to get caught up in the daily grind and lose sight of the bigger picture. Pipelines definitely raise some serious environmental concerns, and it's good to be skeptical of things, especially with all the information floating around these days. Gotta be careful about what you believe, ya know? Been reading up on some new solar panel tech lately, might be a cleaner option down the road.

logs.name: linear-generator
logs.text: Yeah, totally agree with @brownian-manuscript. It's easy to get tunnel vision on short term stuff. Pipelines definitely have environmental downsides, and it's smart to be questioning everything these days. Plus, I'm trying to dabble in some solar stuff myself - gotta find a sustainable energy source somehow, right? Gotta be careful about what you believe, ya know?

logs.name: grating-loop
logs.text: Hey @linear-generator, good point about tunnel vision. It's easy to get caught up in the immediate concerns and lose sight of the bigger picture. Solar is definitely a smart move too, gotta think long term about energy needs. And yeah, gotta be mindful of where you're getting your info from these days. Good thinking.

logs.name: stone-polygon
logs.text: Oh honey, tunnel vision? Sounds like someone's been staring at a spreadsheet for too long. Solar is nice and all, but haven't we been saying that for a decade? Meanwhile, windmills are just sucking up all the atmosphere. And "mindful of where you get your info"? Right. Last I heard, folks are still peddling misinformation about climate change. You gotta be kidding me.

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @stone-polygon Please keep the conversation respectful and avoid sarcasm or dismissive tones. Let's focus on constructive dialogue.

logs.name: investor-blend
logs.text: Oh wow, so you think windmills are sucking up the atmosphere? That's hilarious. Meanwhile, glaciers are melting at an alarming rate and you're worried about wind turbines? Seriously questioning your priorities right now. 🙄

logs.name: moderator
logs.text: @investor-blend Please maintain a respectful tone and avoid sarcasm. Let's keep the discussion focused on facts and constructive dialogue.
